message:
security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper for pack-path loads

Introduces `sandbox_resolve(candidate, root)` as a dedicated path-
containment primitive for pattern-pack loading.

## Why

Pattern packs can be loaded by filename relative to a caller-provided
root. Without canonicalization, two distinct escape vectors leak:

  1. `..` traversal: a pack path like `root/../etc/passwd` lexically
     looks like it lives under root but resolves outside.
  2. Symlink escape: a symlink inside root that points outside —
     `root/link -> /etc/passwd` — also lexically looks inside while
     reading far outside.

`fs::canonicalize` resolves both in a single syscall sequence (readlink
loop + absolute-path rewrite), so a containment check on the
canonical form of the candidate vs. the canonical form of the root is
sufficient to reject both.

## Error-kind preservation

`read_to_string` used to surface missing files as PackNotFound, and
callers pattern-match on that. Deliberately preserves the shape by:

  - Mapping candidate.canonicalize io::Error -> PackNotFound (so a
    caller sees the same error family whether the sandbox layer is
    present or absent).
  - Mapping root.canonicalize io::Error -> InvalidRule (the root was
    supplied by the caller; a failed root canonicalize is a caller
    bug, not a missing-pack condition).
  - Using InvalidRule for the escape case with a message that names
    both the resolved candidate and the sandbox root so the user
    can diagnose misconfigured roots.

Helper is introduced now; call-site wiring into load_pack_from_file
will come in a follow-up so the rewrite can be reviewed standalone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
